summary:
Long-term use of streptomycin can harm the auditory system because it belongs to the aminoglycoside family which targets bacterial proteins; this group includes drugs used for ear conditions where extended treatment increases risk of hearing loss or other related issues. The primary concern with long-duration therapy using such agents lies within their potential to induce irreversible damage along critical pathways like those responsible for sensory perception including audition. Agent2 reasoned based primarily around pharmacological properties shared among specific antibiotic classes affecting cellular structures vital not only during infection but also when exposed over time leading towards possible organ dysfunction especially concerning senses as highlighted here focusing mainly upon ears/auditory function impairment from continuous drug application beyond therapeutic necessity limits set forth clinically speaking.
